steps.title: Find c from the vertical asymptote
steps.content:
The graph does not intersect the line $x = 5$, which means the function is undefined there. The function becomes undefined when its denominator equals zero:
$$2(5) + c = 0$$
$$c = -10$$

steps.title: Find a and b from the zeros
steps.content:
Since $f(6) = 0$ and $f(7) = 0$, and the denominator is nonzero at both points, the numerator must equal zero at $x = 6$ and $x = 7$. The quadratic numerator therefore has roots 6 and 7:
$$x^2 + ax + b = (x - 6)(x - 7)$$
Expanding:
$$(x - 6)(x - 7) = x^2 - 13x + 42$$
Therefore, $a = -13$ and $b = 42$.
